## Hexalith\.EventStore\.Client\.Registration Namespace

| Classes | |
| :--- | :--- |
| [EventStoreActivationContext](Hexalith.EventStore.Client.Registration.EventStoreActivationContext.md 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Client\.Registration\.EventStoreActivationContext') | Holds the runtime activation manifest populated by `UseEventStore()`\. Registered as a singleton during `AddEventStore()` and populated during `UseEventStore()`\. Thread\-safe via [System\.Threading\.Interlocked\.CompareExchange\(System\.Int32@,System\.Int32,System\.Int32\)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.threading.interlocked.compareexchange#system-threading-interlocked-compareexchange(system-int32@-system-int32-system-int32) 'System\.Threading\.Interlocked\.CompareExchange\(System\.Int32@,System\.Int32,System\.Int32\)')\. |
| [EventStoreDomainActivation](Hexalith.EventStore.Client.Registration.EventStoreDomainActivation.md 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Client\.Registration\.EventStoreDomainActivation') | Represents the activation metadata for a single discovered domain, including convention\-derived DAPR resource names\. |
| [EventStoreHostExtensions](Hexalith.EventStore.Client.Registration.EventStoreHostExtensions.md 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Client\.Registration\.EventStoreHostExtensions') | Extension methods on [Microsoft\.Extensions\.Hosting\.IHost](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.extensions.hosting.ihost 'Microsoft\.Extensions\.Hosting\.IHost') for runtime activation of EventStore domain types\. This prepares the activation manifest \(cascade\-resolved DAPR resource names\) — it does NOT directly wire DAPR subscriptions or middleware\. The Client SDK provides the "what" \(which domains exist, their resource names\); the server provides the "how" \(actual DAPR wiring\)\. |
| [EventStoreServiceCollectionExtensions](Hexalith.EventStore.Client.Registration.EventStoreServiceCollectionExtensions.md 'Hexalith\.EventStore\.Client\.Registration\.EventStoreServiceCollectionExtensions') | Extension methods for registering Event Store client services in the dependency injection container\. |
